formations_blocks: Austria enjoying a more settled spell after the penalty, with Argentina struggling to escape pressure
adjustments: Molina advancing from right-back to start Argentina attacks
Messi taking the penalty after Lautaro won it
key_matchups: Molina vs Austria's left-sided defenders in the build-up to the penalty
Lautaro Martínez vs Posch and Schlager in the box
Wanner vs Molina on the left edge of the area
Sabitzer delivering from the left against Argentina's near-post defending
phases.summary: Both teams began on the front foot with a high press, and Argentina tried to progress through Molina’s right-back surges and Messi’s central through-ball. The key moment was a VAR-awarded penalty for Argentina, but Messi missed, after which the game became scrappy and Austria gained a foothold by pressing Argentina back and forcing fouls. Austria’s best spell ended with Wanner winning a foul and Sabitzer floating in a low left-sided delivery that Argentina cleared near the post.

formations_blocks: Austria midfield and pressing phase; attempting to increase tempo and win territory
Argentina operating in a more stop-start, compact defensive posture after taking the lead
adjustments: Austria raised the tempo after conceding
Austria’s pressing and midfield work began generating turnovers and territory but still lacked box entries
key_matchups: Laimer and Sabitzer on the left as Austria’s most potent combination
Danso versus Messi in a late challenge
phases.summary: Argentina’s 38th-minute breakthrough through Messi came from a slick left-sided combination involving Almada and Medina, giving them a 1-0 lead after a phase where Austria had not created much. Austria responded by trying to raise the tempo and use their pressing to win the ball higher, with Laimer and Sabitzer combining well on the left, but they still struggled to turn territory into entries into the box or clear shooting chances. The half also became increasingly fragmented by fouls and late challenges, disrupting any sustained attacking rhythm.

formations_blocks: Argentina defending a 1-0 lead and happy to play out to half-time
Austria unable to penetrate the Argentina back line
Austria showing more hustle and bustle at the start of the second half
phases.summary: Argentina reached half-time 1-0 up and appeared content to protect the lead, with the commentary stressing how difficult Austria found it to break through the Argentine back line. The second half began with Austria showing more energy and a little more attacking presence, but the phase is defined mainly by Argentina’s defensive control and game management.

The phase was defined less by tactical evolution than by Argentina’s control and late finishing, culminating in Messi’s stoppage-time goal to seal a 2-0 win. Austria remained competitive but, as the commentary notes, were unimaginative in attack and were easily contained, with Argentina doing enough to see out the game before adding the late second.
